outlook: Content strategist positions remain stubbornly flat with 1,203 open roles showing zero growth year-over-year and a median salary of $86,000. The moderate AI risk score of 48 reflects the reality that while AI tools like Claude and Perplexity can generate content at scale, they haven't yet mastered the strategic thinking that defines this role. Smart content strategists are doubling down on fundamentally human skills—writing, reading comprehension, and active listening have emerged as the most valuable competencies, while technical skills like GUI development and data mining software have become less relevant. The complete absence of layoff chatter in professional communities suggests the role is stable, even if it's not expanding, as organizations still need humans to determine what content to create and why.

skillPivots.headline: Develop conversational AI prompt engineering
skillPivots.body: Since Claude and Perplexity are your primary displacing tools, learn to master prompt engineering and AI conversation design. Focus on crafting effective prompts that leverage your writing and reading comprehension skills to produce better AI-generated content than competitors.

skillPivots.headline: Transition into user research storytelling
skillPivots.body: Combine your rising active listening skills with strategic content creation to become a user research content specialist. Use your writing expertise to transform user insights into compelling narratives that drive product and marketing decisions.

skillPivots.headline: Build audio-first content strategy expertise
skillPivots.body: Leverage your strong active listening and reading comprehension skills to specialize in podcast strategy, voice user interface content, and audio storytelling. This emerging medium requires deep listening skills and is less susceptible to AI disruption than written content.
